prompt.title: Complete Pydantic request and response models with all enums
prompt.objective: Replace the model stubs from prompt 01 with complete, validated Pydantic v2 schemas for both the POST /analyze-ticket request and response. All enums must match the problem spec exactly — the judge harness checks for exact string values.

tasks.task:
Create a new file app/models/enums.py with these enums (use Python StrEnum or Enum with string values — values must match spec exactly, case-sensitive):
en | bn | mixed
in_app_chat | call_center | email | merchant_portal | field_agent
customer | merchant | agent | unknown
transfer | payment | cash_in | cash_out | settlement | refund
completed | failed | pending | reversed
wrong_transfer | payment_failed | refund_request | duplicate_payment | merchant_settlement_delay | agent_cash_in_issue | phishing_or_social_engineering | other
low | medium | high | critical
customer_support | dispute_resolution | payments_ops | merchant_operations | agent_operations | fraud_risk
consistent | inconsistent | insufficient_data

Replace the stub with a full Pydantic v2 BaseModel:
TransactionEntry: transaction_id : str timestamp : str # ISO 8601; store as str, validate format type : TransactionType amount : float # BDT, must be > 0 counterparty : str status : TransactionStatus
AnalyzeTicketRequest: ticket_id : str # required complaint : str # required; min length 1 language : Optional[Language] # default None channel : Optional[Channel] # default None user_type : Optional[UserType] # default None campaign_context : Optional[str] # default None transaction_history : Optional[List[TransactionEntry]] = [] metadata : Optional[Dict[str, Any]] # default None
Validation rules: - ticket_id must be non-empty string. - complaint must be non-empty string (strip whitespace before checking). - amount in TransactionEntry must be positive. - timestamp in TransactionEntry must loosely match ISO 8601 (regex check: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ or with offset).

Replace the stub with:
AnalyzeTicketResponse: ticket_id : str relevant_transaction_id : Optional[str] # null if no match evidence_verdict : EvidenceVerdict case_type : CaseType severity : Severity department : Department agent_summary : str recommended_next_action : str customer_reply : str human_review_required : bool confidence : Optional[float] = None # 0.0 – 1.0 reason_codes : Optional[List[str]] = []
Add a validator: if confidence is provided, it must be in [0.0, 1.0].
ErrorResponse (for 400 / 500): error : str detail : Optional[str] = None

Update the POST /analyze-ticket stub to: - Accept body typed as AnalyzeTicketRequest (Pydantic will auto-validate). - On valid input, still return { "status": "not_implemented" } for now. - FastAPI will now automatically return HTTP 422 for schema violations (this satisfies the optional 422 requirement in the spec).

Write pytest tests covering: a) Valid full request parses without error. b) Missing ticket_id raises ValidationError. c) Empty complaint string raises ValidationError. d) Invalid CaseType value raises ValidationError. e) Negative transaction amount raises ValidationError. f) Confidence = 1.5 in response raises ValidationError. g) All enum values round-trip correctly (serialize → deserialize → same value).

prompt.verification: Run: pytest tests/ -v All tests from prompt 01 and new schema tests must pass.
prompt.do_not: - Do not rename or change any enum string values — they must match the spec verbatim. - Do not make optional fields required. - Do not use int for amount — use float to handle decimal BDT values.
